content: Shibboleth's FileBackedHTTPMetadataProvider fetches federation metadata over HTTP and spools it to a local backing file, used ONLY at cold-start (a live refresh never falls back to the backing file — it can't be newer than what's already in memory). Refresh cadence is governed by minRefreshDelay (default PT30S) / maxRefreshDelay (default PT4H) plus the metadata's own cacheDuration/validUntil, and refreshes run in a background thread so they don't block request handling. In egress-restricted clusters, the SP process needs an explicit egress allowlist to the metadata URL (or an HTTP proxy config) or it must be pointed at a purely local/pre-fetched metadata file (no periodic refresh) — an in-cluster metadata mirror/cache that itself does the periodic HTTP fetch and exposes a local URL is the standard way to reconcile 'stay current with federation metadata' against 'no general egress.'
